titles.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُوصِي بِوَصِيَّةٍ ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ عَنْهَا
titles.en: Chapter 8 – The man bequeaths by his bequest, then retracts from it

titles.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُوصِي بِوَصِيَّةٍ ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ عَنْهَا
titles.en: Chapter 8 – The man bequeaths by his bequest, then retracts from it

segments: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ لِلْمُوصِي أَنْ يَرْجِعَ فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ إِنْ كَانَ فِي صِحَّةٍ أَوْ مَرَضٍ .
arabic.text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ لِلْمُوصِي أَنْ يَرْجِعَ فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ إِنْ كَانَ فِي صِحَّةٍ أَوْ مَرَضٍ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ibn Bakeyr, from Ubeyd Bin Zarara who said,
‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying to the testator that he can retract (draw back) in his bequest whether it was during good health or illness’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ibn Bakeyr, from Ubeyd Bin Zarara who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying to the testator that he can retract (draw back) in his bequest whether it was during good health or illness’.

segments: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from ibn Bukayr from ‘Ubayd ibn Zurarah who has narrated the following: “I once heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ’One who makes a will has the right to cancel or leave it, regardless of his being in good health or in illness.
en.sarwar.text: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from ibn Bukayr from ‘Ubayd ibn Zurarah who has narrated the following: “I once heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ’One who makes a will has the right to cancel or leave it, regardless of his being in good health or in illness.

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/22)
gradings.label: حسن أو موثق
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/22)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنِ ابْنِ بُكَيْرٍ عَنْ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ زُرَارَةَ قَالَ

titles.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُوصِي بِوَصِيَّةٍ ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ عَنْهَا
titles.en: Chapter 8 – The man bequeaths by his bequest, then retracts from it

segments: لِصَاحِبِ الْوَصِيَّةِ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ فِيهَا وَ يُحْدِثَ فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ مَا دَامَ حَيّاً .
arabic.text: لِصَاحِبِ الْوَصِيَّةِ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ فِيهَا وَ يُحْدِثَ فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ مَا دَامَ حَيّاً .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ibn Fazal, from Ali Bin Uqba, from Bureyd Al Ajaly,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said to the owner of the bequest (Testator) that he can retract it in, and add something new in his bequest, for as long as he is alive’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ibn Fazal, from Ali Bin Uqba, from Bureyd Al Ajaly,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said to the owner of the bequest (Testator) that he can retract it in, and add something new in his bequest, for as long as he is alive’.

segments: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from ibn Faddal from Ali ibn ‘Uqbah from Burayd al-‘Ijliy who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ’The testator has the right to cancel or change his will in his life time.”’
en.sarwar.text: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from ibn Faddal from Ali ibn ‘Uqbah from Burayd al-‘Ijliy who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ’The testator has the right to cancel or change his will in his life time.”’

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/22)
gradings.label: موثق
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/22)

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنِ ابْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عُقْبَةَ عَنْ بُرَيْدٍ الْعِجْلِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُوصِي بِوَصِيَّةٍ ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ عَنْهَا
titles.en: Chapter 8 – The man bequeaths by his bequest, then retracts from it

segments: قَضَى أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّ الْمُدَبَّرَ مِنَ الثُّلُثِ وَ أَنَّ لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يَنْقُضَ وَصِيَّتَهُ فَيَزِيدَ فِيهَا وَ يَنْقُصَ مِنْهَا مَا لَمْ يَمُتْ .
arabic.text: قَضَى أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّ الْمُدَبَّرَ مِنَ الثُّلُثِ وَ أَنَّ لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يَنْقُضَ وَصِيَّتَهُ فَيَزِيدَ فِيهَا وَ يَنْقُصَ مِنْهَا مَا لَمْ يَمُتْ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from Ibn Muskan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineenasws judged that the Mudabbar (slave to be emancipated (liberated) upon the death of the master), is from the third; and it is up to the man that he invalidates his bequest, so he can increase in it, and decrease from it, for as long as he had not died’.
en.hubeali.text: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from Ibn Muskan, (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineenasws judged that the Mudabbar (slave to be emancipated (liberated) upon the death of the master), is from the third; and it is up to the man that he invalidates his bequest, so he can increase in it, and decrease from it, for as long as he had not died’.

segments: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from ibn Muskan who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin issued a judgment about al- Mudabbar (slave to be set free with the death of the owner) as being part of one-third of the legacy and that a testator has the right to reduce or increase in his will as long as he is alive.’”
en.sarwar.text: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from ibn Muskan who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin issued a judgment about al- Mudabbar (slave to be set free with the death of the owner) as being part of one-third of the legacy and that a testator has the right to reduce or increase in his will as long as he is alive.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/23)
gradings.label: صحيح
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/23)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ يُونُسَ عَنِ ابْنِ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُوصِي بِوَصِيَّةٍ ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ عَنْهَا
titles.en: Chapter 8 – The man bequeaths by his bequest, then retracts from it

segments: قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يُغَيِّرَ وَصِيَّتَهُ فَيُعْتِقَ مَنْ كَانَ أَمَرَ بِمِلْكِهِ وَ يُمَلِّكَ مَنْ كَانَ أَمَرَ بِعِتْقِهِ وَ يُعْطِيَ مَنْ كَانَ حَرَمَهُ وَ يَحْرِمَ مَنْ كَانَ أَعْطَاهُ مَا لَمْ يَمُتْ .
arabic.text: قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يُغَيِّرَ وَصِيَّتَهُ فَيُعْتِقَ مَنْ كَانَ أَمَرَ بِمِلْكِهِ وَ يُمَلِّكَ مَنْ كَانَ أَمَرَ بِعِتْقِهِ وَ يُعْطِيَ مَنْ كَانَ حَرَمَهُ وَ يَحْرِمَ مَنْ كَانَ أَعْطَاهُ مَا لَمْ يَمُتْ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from one of his companions who said,

‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It is up to the man that he changes his bequest, so he frees the one who had been ordered to be kept as a slave, and enslaves the one whom he had ordered to be freed, and give to the one whom he had forbidden it, and forbid the one whom he had given to, for as long as he has not died’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from one of his companions who said,

‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It is up to the man that he changes his bequest, so he frees the one who had been ordered to be kept as a slave, and enslaves the one whom he had ordered to be freed, and give to the one whom he had forbidden it, and forbid the one whom he had given to, for as long as he has not died’.

segments: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from certain persons of his people who has narrated the following: “Ali ibn al-Husayn, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, has said, ‘A testator has the right to change his will, thus he can set free those whom he had willed to remain slaves or keep the slaves those that were freed in his will; give those who were deprived and deprive those whom he had given as long as he is alive.’”
en.sarwar.text: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from certain persons of his people who has narrated the following: “Ali ibn al-Husayn, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, has said, ‘A testator has the right to change his will, thus he can set free those whom he had willed to remain slaves or keep the slaves those that were freed in his will; give those who were deprived and deprive those whom he had given as long as he is alive.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/23)
gradings.label: مرسل
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/23)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ يُونُسَ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِهِ قَالَ
